name: awkward-kaitai

metadata.name: awkward_kaitai
metadata.summary: Kaitai Struct runtime for Awkward Arrays

documentation: # Kaitai Struct: runtime library for Awkward¥n¥nThis library building Awkward Arrays using Kaitai Struct API for Awkward using¥nC++/STL.¥n¥n## Steps¥n¥n### 1. Write a .ksy file for your custom file format. Refer to the [Kaitai User Guide](https://doc.kaitai.io/user_guide.html) for more details.¥n¥nHere, we take an example of `animal.ksy`¥n¥n```yaml¥nmeta:¥n  id: animal¥n  endian: le¥n  license: CC0-1.0¥n  ks-version: 0.8¥n¥nseq:¥n  - id: entry¥n    type: animal_entry¥n    repeat: eos¥n¥ntypes:¥n  animal_entry:¥n    seq:¥n      - id: str_len¥n        type: u1¥n¥n      - id: species¥n        type: str¥n        size: str_len¥n        encoding: UTF-8¥n¥n      - id: age¥n        type: u1¥n¥n      - id: weight¥n        type: u2¥n```¥n¥n### 2. Clone `kaitai_struct_awkward_runtime` repository:¥n¥n```¥ngit clone --recursive https://github.com/det-lab/kaitai_struct_awkward_runtime.git¥n```¥n¥n```¥ncd kaitai_struct_awkward_runtime¥n```¥n```¥ngit checkout ManasviGoyal/test¥n```¥n¥n### 3. Update submodule and compile scala code (only the first time)¥n¥n```¥ngit submodule update --init¥n```¥n¥n```¥ncd kaitai_struct_compiler¥n```¥n¥n```¥nsbt package¥n```¥n¥n```¥ncd ../¥nchmod u+x kaitai-struct-compiler¥n```¥n¥n### 4. Generate the source and header files for Awkward target¥n¥n```¥n./kaitai-struct-compiler -t awkward --outdir src-animal example_data/schemas/animal.ksy¥n```¥n¥n### 5. Install the library¥n```¥npip install awkward-kaitai¥n```¥n¥n### 6. Build `awkward-kaitai` by passing the path of the main `.cpp` from the generated code.¥nIf python gives a warning about the installation not being on the path, in order to get the file to build you may need to run:¥n```¥necho ¥

>> ‾/.bashrc¥n```¥n¥n```¥nawkward-kaitai-build src-animal/animal.cpp -b build¥n```¥n> **Note:**¥n>¥n> `awkward-kaitai-build [-h] [-d DEST] [-b BUILD] file`¥n>¥n> options:¥n>- `-h, --help`: shows help message¥n>- `-d DEST, --dest DEST`: explicitly specify a destination for the build shared library.¥n>- `-b BUILD, --build BUILD`: explicitly specify a build location.¥n¥n### 7. Open python and print the returned `ak.Array`:¥n```¥npython¥n```¥n¥n```python¥nimport awkward_kaitai¥n¥nanimal = awkward_kaitai.Reader(¥
) # pass the path of the shared file¥nawkward_array = animal.load(¥

)¥n¥nawkward_array.to_list()¥n```¥n¥n#### Output¥n¥n```python¥n[{'animalA__Zentry': [{'animal_entryA__Zstr_len': 3, 'animal_entryA__Zspecies': 'cat', 'animal_entryA__Zage': 5, 'animal_entryA__Zweight': 12}, {'animal_entryA__Zstr_len': 3, 'animal_entryA__Zspecies': 'dog', 'animal_entryA__Zage': 3, 'animal_entryA__Zweight': 43}, {'animal_entryA__Zstr_len': 6, 'animal_entryA__Zspecies': 'turtle', 'animal_entryA__Zage': 10, 'animal_entryA__Zweight': 5}]}]¥n```¥n¥n## Related Papers and Talks¥n1. [Describe Data to get Science-Data-Ready Tooling: Awkward as a Target for Kaitai Struct YAML](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1330797/contributions/5796564/), Advanced Computing and Analysis Techniques for Physics Research Workshop 2024, New York, US.¥n2. [Awkward Target for Kaitai Struct](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1252095/contributions/5592420/), PyHEP Users Workshop 2023.¥n
